title: Success Criteria, Baselines, and Prompt Evals · Contract Clinic: Success Criteria
summary: Define and test a support-triage prompt before optimizing its wording. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt success criteria increment in a versioned prompt evaluation harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Turn subjective quality goals into observable criteria and representative test cases.
Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A prompt is good when one impressive example works. This fails the Success Criteria contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Success Criteria evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-success-criteria-r1kctp" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Success Criteria into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-success-criteria-r1kctp
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
